content:
𝐄𝐯𝐞𝐫𝐲𝐨𝐧𝐞 𝐢𝐬 𝐭𝐚𝐥𝐤𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐚𝐛𝐨𝐮𝐭 𝐂𝐥𝐚𝐮𝐝𝐞 𝐒𝐨𝐧𝐧𝐞𝐭 𝟓, 𝐛𝐮𝐭 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐫𝐞𝐚𝐥 𝐪𝐮𝐞𝐬𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧 𝐢𝐬 𝐭𝐡𝐢𝐬: 𝐰𝐡𝐚𝐭 𝐚𝐜𝐭𝐮𝐚𝐥𝐥𝐲 𝐜𝐡𝐚𝐧𝐠𝐞𝐝, 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐝𝐨𝐞𝐬 𝐢𝐭 𝐦𝐚𝐭𝐭𝐞𝐫 𝐟𝐨𝐫 𝐝𝐞𝐯𝐞𝐥𝐨𝐩𝐞𝐫𝐬?

From improved coding performance and stronger reasoning to updated API defaults, pricing changes, benchmark results, and a clear comparison with Opus 4.8 and Haiku 4.5, there's a lot to unpack before deciding where it fits in your AI stack.

I broke everything down into one practical, developer-friendly guide so you can understand what's new without spending hours reading release notes.

Read the full blog here: https://www.orbittest.dev/blog/claude-sonnet-5-explained

What do you think—will Claude Sonnet 5 become your default coding assistant, or are you sticking with another model?
